_comment_rss_feeds: Add one entry per Google Alert. Create alerts at google.com/alerts using the site:-targeted strings in the README, set 'Deliver to: RSS feed', then paste the feed URL below. See the Google Alerts Setup section of README.md for the recommended 9 alert strings.
_comment_resume_path: Full or relative path to your resume .docx file. Examples: '/Users/yourname/Documents/Resume.docx' or 'Resume.docx' if it's in the same folder as app.py.
_comment_narrative_path: Optional. Full or relative path to a second .docx document (e.g. a cover letter, LinkedIn summary, or career narrative). Not used in scoring today — reserved for a future context-aware feature.
_comment_resume_keywords: Auto-generated on first launch — do not edit. The app extracts keywords from your resume and caches them here so startup is fast. To force a re-scan (e.g. after updating your resume), delete everything between the brackets and save: []
_comment_custom_keywords: Domain-specific multi-word phrases to inject directly into your ATS keyword set — useful for niche terms your resume may not spell out explicitly (e.g. 'mls integration', 'p95 latency', 'platform product manager'). Single words work too. These are merged with your extracted resume keywords and matched against every job posting.
_comment_custom_synonyms: Synonym pairs that let the scorer treat variant spellings as the same keyword. Each entry is a two-element list: [variant, canonical]. Example: ["mls", "multiple listing service"] means postings containing either phrase count as a match. Variants are case-insensitive.
_comment_user_profile: Controls the Fit Score (0-10) shown on each job. 'titles' should list your target job titles from most to least preferred — the scorer checks if the posting title contains any of them. 'years_experience' is your total relevant experience used to judge seniority fit. 'preferred_domains' boosts scores for industries you want — common values: saas, api, platform, proptech, fintech, edtech, healthtech.
_comment_job_filters: Controls which jobs are silently dropped before reaching your board. 'require_us_only' drops postings that mention non-US locations. 'allow_remote' always lets remote roles through regardless of location. 'onsite_regions' lists cities or regions you are willing to commute to for hybrid/onsite roles — leave as [] to accept all locations.
_comment_blocked_domains: Domains to permanently exclude from your feed. Any URL whose hostname contains one of these strings is dropped before processing. Add trade publications, news sites, or any other source producing noise in your results.
_comment_app: Server settings. 'port' is the local port Job Radar runs on (default 5000 — change if something else is using that port). Set 'debug' to true only when developing; it causes the server to auto-reload on code changes.
_comment_github_repo: Your GitHub repository in 'username/repo-name' format. Used by the in-app update checker to compare your local VERSION against the latest release. Example: 'johndoe/job-radar'. Leave as-is if you did not clone from GitHub.
_comment_ats_domains: Maps URL fragments to platform names shown in the UI. The app checks if a posting URL contains any of these strings to identify the platform. Special behavior: LinkedIn postings are stored without scores (login wall) and show a notice to navigate directly to the listing. Indeed and ZipRecruiter postings are scraped normally — the app attempts to find the direct ATS link on the page and upgrades the URL if found.
_comment_fit_score_weights: Maximum points each factor contributes to the Fit Score (0-10 total). title_match: how well the job title matches your target titles. skills_coverage: derived from your ATS keyword match percentage. experience_level: how well the posted years-of-experience requirement aligns with yours. domain_fit: industry/domain alignment with your preferred_domains. work_style: remote/hybrid/onsite preference match.
_comment_ats_score_weights: Controls how the ATS keyword match percentage (0-100%) is calculated. 'required_skills' are keywords that appear in mandatory sections of the posting. 'preferred_skills' appear in nice-to-have sections. 'title_match' gives a small boost when your resume titles appear in the posting. The three weights should add up to 1.0.
